title: Restricting Tool Access
concept: Sandboxing and restricting tool execution to prevent unauthorized changes to the host environment.

exercises.prompt: Quick recap! Why does writing a clear docstring for a tool help an AI agent?

options.text: It tells the agent what the tool does and when to use it, so it picks the right one.
It makes the tool run faster on the computer.
It hides the tool so the agent can never see it.

exercises.explain: A good docstring is like a clear label. When the agent reads it, it understands the tool's purpose and picks the correct tool for the job. Clear docs = smarter choices.

cards.title: A Powerful Helper Needs Guardrails
cards.body: An AI agent can run tools that change real files, send emails, or delete data. That power is useful, but it can also cause accidents. So we put up guardrails.

cards.title: What Is Restricting Tool Access?
cards.body: Restricting tool access means limiting what an agent's tools are allowed to do. Sandboxing runs the tools inside a safe, walled-off space so they can't harm the main computer.

cards.title: Like a Science Lab Fume Hood
cards.body: In chemistry class you mix risky chemicals inside a sealed fume hood, not on your desk. A sandbox is that hood for AI: experiments stay contained and can't spill into the real system.

cards.title: A Real Example
cards.body: An agent is asked to organize photos. With sandboxing, it can only touch the 'Photos' folder. Even if it makes a mistake, it can't delete your homework or system files elsewhere.

cards.title: Myth: Restricting Tools Makes Agents Useless
cards.body: Not true! Limiting access doesn't remove an agent's skills. It just keeps it inside safe boundaries, so it does its job without risking damage.

cards.title: Open Access vs. Sandboxed
cards.body: The same agent, two setups. One can touch anything; the other stays in a safe zone.

left.text: Agent can edit or delete ANY file on the computer. One bug could wreck everything.

right.text: Agent only works inside a walled-off space. Mistakes stay contained and harmless.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of sandboxing an AI agent's tools?

options.text: To keep tool actions contained so they can't damage the host system.
To make the agent respond with longer answers.
To give the agent access to every file on the computer.
To delete the agent's memory after each task.

exercises.explain: Sandboxing walls off the agent's tools inside a safe space. If something goes wrong, the damage stays inside that space and can't reach the rest of the host system.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Restricting tool access means an agent can never do anything useful.
exercises.explain: False! Restricting access just sets safe boundaries. The agent keeps all its abilities inside those limits and can still complete its tasks safely.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about safe tool execution.
exercises.sentence: Running an agent's tools inside a walled-off space is called sandboxing.
exercises.explain: Sandboxing is the word for keeping tool actions inside a contained, safe space so they can't harm the host environment.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of sandboxing sounds like it was AI-generated (smooth and complete)?

options.text: Sandboxing runs an agent's tools in an isolated environment, so any unauthorized or accidental actions are contained and cannot alter the host system.
sandbox is like when u play in the sand idk it just keeps stuff safe maybe
Sandbox good. Tool no break computer. Yes safe.

exercises.explain: Option A is smooth, complete, and uses precise terms like 'isolated environment' and 'contained' - a polished, AI-style explanation. The others are casual or broken.

exercises.prompt: An agent is asked to clean up temporary files. You want it to do the job but you're worried it might delete important system files by mistake. What's the safest setup?

options.text: Give the agent full access to the entire computer so it works fastest.
Sandbox the agent so its delete tool can only touch the temporary-files folder.
Remove all of the agent's tools so it can't do anything at all.
Let it run freely and just hope it doesn't make a mistake.

exercises.explain: The safest choice is B: sandbox the agent so its tools are restricted to only the temporary-files folder. This lets the agent finish the task while making sure a mistake can't reach important system files. Great job finishing Unit 490 - you now know how agents pick the right tool AND how to keep those tools safe with restricted, sandboxed access!
